channel.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Edgar's Personal Wiki

item.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
item.description: Edgar’s Personal Wiki Public notebook This is the public edge of a private working wiki: source-backed notes about software, systems, books, institutions, and the moments where fiction explains reality better than reality explains itself.

item.title: ai-commissioning
item.description: AI Commissioning AI commissioning is the shift from using an AI system as a closely steered assistant to briefing an agentic model on an ambitious outcome, letting it execute through a long and partly hidden workflow, and then judging the delivered artifact.

item.title: ai-era-engineering-apprenticeship
item.description: AI-Era Engineering Apprenticeship AI-era engineering apprenticeship is the user’s emerging question about how people develop judgment when AI agents absorb more of the small execution work that previously trained juniors and mid-level engineers.

item.title: machine-usefulness
item.description: Machine Usefulness Machine usefulness is Power and Progress’s alternative to the machine-intelligence frame.

item.title: recursive-self-improvement
item.description: Recursive Self-Improvement Recursive self-improvement is the threshold where an AI system can autonomously design and develop its own successor.

item.title: technology-and-worker-power
item.description: Technology and Worker Power Technology and worker power is the core institutional thesis from Power and Progress: productivity growth becomes shared prosperity only when workers have enough bargaining, political, and social power to shape technology’s direction and claim part of its gains.

item.title: dario-amodei
item.description: Dario Amodei Dario Amodei enters the wiki through Policy on the AI Exponential, a June 2026 essay on AI policy, safety regulation, labor displacement, scientific acceleration, civil liberties, and geopolitics.

item.title: ethan-mollick
item.description: Ethan Mollick Ethan Mollick is the credited author of the One Useful Thing article What it feels like to work with Mythos.

item.title: democratic-power-and-geopolitics
item.description: Democratic Power and Geopolitics Democratic power and geopolitics is the post-AI question of whether advanced AI strengthens accountable democracies or concentrates power in states, companies, and autocracies.

item.title: index
item.description: Post-AI World Post-AI world is this wiki’s working section for the social, institutional, and personal consequences of advanced AI becoming a central economic and political force.
